display.text:
model: gemma-4-26b
task: generate_mujoco_xml

instructions: |
  Generate a valid MuJoCo XML model of a simple ideal pendulum using SI units.

  Coordinate system:
    +x = right
    +y = upward
    +z = out of page

  Pendulum definition:
    - Massless rod
    - Spherical bob
    - Single hinge joint

  Joint specifications:
    - Type: hinge
    - Location: [0, 1, 0]
    - Axis: [0, 0, 1]
    - Damping: 0
    - Friction: 0
    - No losses

  Rod:
    - Length: 0.2485 m
    - Mass: 0
    - Collision: disabled

  Bob:
    - Shape: sphere
    - Diameter: 0.1 m
    - Mass: 1.0 kg

  Environment:
    - Gravity: [0, -9.81, 0]

  Initial conditions:
    - Pendulum starts horizontal
    - Bob positioned along +x from hinge
    - Initial angle: +90 degrees from vertical
    - Initial angular velocity: 0 rad/s

  MuJoCo requirements:
    - Enable energy computation
    - Exactly one hinge joint
    - Joint rotates only about z-axis
    - Use SI units

  Acceptance criteria:
    - Exactly one hinge joint
    - Rod length = 0.2485 m
    - Rod mass = 0 kg
    - Bob mass = 1 kg
    - Gravity = [0, -9.81, 0]
    - Initial angle = +90 degrees
    - Initial angular velocity = 0
    - Energy computation enabled

output_format:
  type: xml
  constraints:
    - Return only valid MuJoCo XML
    - No explanations
    - No additional text

state.title: YAML_creation/Temperature_Testing/V7_Temp_0.2/test14/ChatGPT-5.3-Instant_Pend.YAML

input.content:
<mujoco>
  <option gravity="0 0 -9.81" integrator="Euler" />
  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 3" dir="0 0 -1"/>
    <body name="pendulum" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.01" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      <body name="bob" pos="0.2485 0 0">
        <geom name="sphere" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>
</mujoco>

<mujoco model="pendulum">
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" />

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 3" dir="0 0 -1"/>
    
    <body name="hinge_body" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge_joint" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      
      <!-- Massless rod -->
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.001" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      
      <body name="bob_body" pos="0.2485 0 0">
        <!-- Spherical bob: diameter 0.1m -> radius 0.05m -->
        <geom name="bob" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>

  <!-- Initial conditions: Pendulum starts horizontal (+90 deg from vertical) -->
  <!-- Since +y is upward and gravity is [0, -9.81, 0], vertical is [0, 1, 0] -->
  <!-- Horizontal is [1, 0, 0] or [-1, 0, 0]. The prompt says bob positioned along +x from hinge. -->
  <!-- To set initial state in MuJoCo XML, we use <key> or <qpos> if using a model with state. 
       However, for a simple XML, we can set the initial pos/quat or use <key>. 
       Actually, MuJoCo XML doesn't support <qpos> directly in the <body/joint> tag for initial state in all versions, 
       but we can use <key> or just provide the XML.
       The prompt asks to "Generate a valid MuJoCo XML model".
       Wait, the prompt says "Pendulum starts horizontal" and "Bob positioned along +x from hinge". 
       If the joint is at (0,1,0) and the bob is at (0.2485, 0, 0) relative to the joint, 
       the bob's global position is (0.2485, 1, 0). 
       If the joint is at (0,1,0) and the axis is (0,0,1), then the rotation is in the XY plane.
       Gravity is (0, -9.81, 0).
       If the bob is at (0.2485, 1, 0), it is indeed horizontal.
  -->

</mujoco>

<mujoco model="pendulum">
  <option gravity="0 -9.81 0" />

  <worldbody>
    <light diffuse=".5 .5 .5" pos="0 0 3" dir="0 0 -1"/>
    
    <body name="hinge_body" pos="0 1 0">
      <joint name="hinge_joint" type="hinge" axis="0 0 1" damping="0" friction="0"/>
      
      <!-- Massless rod -->
      <geom name="rod" type="capsule" fromto="0 0 0 0.2485 0 0" size="0.001" mass="0" contype="0" conaffinity="0"/>
      
      <body name="bob_body" pos="0.2485 0 0">
        <!-- Spherical bob: diameter 0.1m -> radius 0.05m -->
        <geom name="bob" type="sphere" size="0.05" mass="1.0"/>
      </body>
    </body>
  </worldbody>
</mujoco>
